posts.title: Why Math.random() is Never Acceptable for Password Generation
posts.author: Alex Rivera, Staff Security Engineer
posts.excerpt: Math.random() uses a Mersenne Twister PRNG — statistically predictable after observing 624 outputs. Here is why every security-sensitive password generation routine must use crypto.getRandomValues() instead.

posts.title: Building a Cryptographically Secure Password API in Node.js and Python
posts.author: Alex Rivera, Staff Security Engineer
posts.excerpt: A complete walkthrough of building a production-grade password generation API using Node.js crypto and Python secrets modules, with rate limiting, API key authentication, and structured JSON output.

posts.title: Secrets Management in CI/CD: How to Stop Hardcoding Credentials
posts.author: Alex Rivera, Staff Security Engineer
posts.excerpt: Hardcoded credentials in CI/CD pipelines are the most common secret leak vector. This guide covers GitHub Actions secrets, HashiCorp Vault dynamic credentials, and the correct pattern for generating and injecting passwords at runtime.

posts.title: Password Generation at Scale: Patterns for IAM Provisioning Pipelines
posts.author: Alex Rivera, Staff Security Engineer
posts.excerpt: Provisioning thousands of user accounts requires deterministic, auditable, policy-compliant password generation. This guide covers bulk generation patterns, CSV output pipelines, and safe handoff to Active Directory and cloud IAM.

posts.title: Rate Limiting Credential Endpoints: Patterns and Implementation
posts.author: Alex Rivera, Staff Security Engineer
posts.excerpt: Any endpoint that generates or validates credentials is a brute-force target. This guide implements token bucket rate limiting in Express, nginx, and API Gateway with Redis-backed distributed state.

posts.title: From Dev to Prod: Secure Password Generation Across Environments
posts.author: Alex Rivera, Staff Security Engineer
posts.excerpt: Local dev, staging, and production need different credential strategies. Covering dotenv patterns, secret rotation on deploy, environment parity without secret sprawl, and the 12-factor app approach to credential management.
